## Discussion

The name of the new asset group is `name`, its type is [doc://com.apple.assetslibrary/documentation/AssetsLibrary/ALAssetsGroupAlbum], and the [doc://com.apple.assetslibrary/documentation/AssetsLibrary/ALAssetsGroup/isEditable] property is [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true].

This method is asynchronous. When the assets group is added, the user may be asked to confirm the application’s access to the data; the method, though, returns immediately. You should perform whatever work you want with the group in `resultBlock`.

If the user denies access to the application, or if no application is allowed to access the data, or if the data is currently unavailable, the failure block is called.

# addAssetsGroupAlbum(withName:resultBlock:failureBlock:)

**Instance Method**

Adds a new assets group to the library.

## Declaration

```swift
func addAssetsGroupAlbum(withName name: String!, resultBlock: ALAssetsLibraryGroupResultBlock!, failureBlock: ALAssetsLibraryAccessFailureBlock!)
```

## Parameters

- **name**: The name for the new group.

If `name` conflicts with another assets group with the same name, then the group is not created and `resultBlock` returns a `nil` group.
- **resultBlock**: The block invoked after the add operation completes.

For a description of the block, see [doc://com.apple.assetslibrary/documentation/AssetsLibrary/ALAssetsLibraryAccessFailureBlock].
- **failureBlock**: The block to invoke if the add operation fails—for example, if the user denies access to the application.

For a description of the block, see [doc://com.apple.assetslibrary/documentation/AssetsLibrary/ALAssetsGroupFaces].

## Discussion

The name of the new asset group is `name`, its type is [doc://com.apple.assetslibrary/documentation/AssetsLibrary/ALAssetsGroupAlbum], and the [doc://com.apple.assetslibrary/documentation/AssetsLibrary/ALAssetsGroup/isEditable] property is [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true].

This method is asynchronous. When the assets group is added, the user may be asked to confirm the application’s access to the data; the method, though, returns immediately. You should perform whatever work you want with the group in `resultBlock`.

If the user denies access to the application, or if no application is allowed to access the data, or if the data is currently unavailable, the failure block is called.

## Managing Asset Groups

- **group(for:resultBlock:failureBlock:)**: Returns an assets group in the result block for a URL previously retrieved from an `ALAssetsGroup` object.
